channel.title: Check on FsFlow
channel.description: Recent content in Check on FsFlow

item.title: Check.
item.description:
Returns success when both checks succeed.

Signature

Check.``and`` left right

Parameters

Name Type Description
left Check<’left> The first check.
right Check<‘right> The second check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds only if both inputs succeed.

Remarks

This is a logical "and" operation. It short-circuits: if left fails, right is not evaluated.

Examples

 Check.and (Check.okIf true) (Check.okIf true) // Ok ()
 Check.and (Check.okIf true) (Check.okIf false) // Error ()

item.title: Check.
item.description:
Returns success when the supplied check fails.

Signature

Check.``not`` check

Parameters

Name Type Description
check Check<‘value> The source Check to invert.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the input fails; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Remarks

This is a logical "not" operation for checks. Note that it discards the success value and returns Unit on success.

Examples

 Check.okIf true |> Check.not // Error ()
 Check.okIf false |> Check.not // Ok ()

item.title: Check.
item.description:
Returns success when either check succeeds.

Signature

Check.``or`` left right

Parameters

Name Type Description
left Check<’left> The first check.
right Check<‘right> The second check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if either input succeeds.

Remarks

This is a logical "or" operation. It short-circuits: if left succeeds, right is not evaluated.

Examples

 Check.or (Check.okIf true) (Check.okIf false) // Ok ()
 Check.or (Check.okIf false) (Check.okIf false) // Error ()

item.title: Check.all
item.description:
Returns success when every check in the sequence succeeds.

Signature

Check.all checks

Parameters

Name Type Description
checks Check<‘value> seq A sequence of checks.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds only if all inputs succeed.

Remarks

Sequentially evaluates each check in the checks sequence. Stops at the first failure.

Examples

 [ Check.okIf true; Check.okIf true ] |> Check.all // Ok ()
 [ Check.okIf true; Check.okIf false ] |> Check.all // Error ()

item.title: Check.any
item.description:
Returns success when at least one check in the sequence succeeds.

Signature

Check.any checks

Parameters

Name Type Description
checks Check<‘value> seq A sequence of checks.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if any input succeeds.

Remarks

Sequentially evaluates each check in the checks sequence. Stops at the first success.

Examples

 [ Check.okIf false; Check.okIf true ] |> Check.any // Ok ()
 [ Check.okIf false; Check.okIf false ] |> Check.any // Error ()

item.title: Check.blank
item.description:
Returns success when the string is blank.

Signature

Check.blank str

Parameters

Name Type Description
str string The string to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the string is blank; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 "  " |> Check.blank // Ok ()
 "hello" |> Check.blank // Error ()

item.title: Check
item.description:
Predicate helpers that return Result values with a unit error, plus the bridge functions that turn those checks into application errors. Some helpers preserve the source value; others are gates and return unit on success.

Signature

type Check

item.title: Check.equal
item.description:
Returns success when the values are equal.

Signature

Check.equal expected actual

Parameters

Name Type Description
expected ‘a The expected value.
actual ‘a The actual value.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the values are equal; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 Check.equal 5 5 // Ok ()
 Check.equal 5 6 // Error ()

item.title: Check.failIf
item.description:
Returns success when the condition is false.

Signature

Check.failIf cond

Parameters

Name Type Description
cond bool The boolean condition to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if cond is false.

Examples

 Check.failIf (1 = 2) // Ok ()
 Check.failIf (1 = 1) // Error ()

item.title: Check.failIfBlank
item.description:
Returns the string when it is blank.

Signature

Check.failIfBlank str

Parameters

Name Type Description
str string The string to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<string> A Check containing the blank string; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 "  " |> Check.failIfBlank // Ok "  "
 "hello" |> Check.failIfBlank // Error ()

item.title: Check.failIfEmpty
item.description:
Returns the sequence when it is not empty.

Signature

Check.failIfEmpty coll

Parameters

Name Type Description
coll ‘a seq The sequence to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<‘a seq> A Check containing the non-empty sequence; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 [1] |> Check.failIfEmpty // Ok [1]
 [] |> Check.failIfEmpty // Error ()

item.title: Check.failIfEmptyStr
item.description:
Returns the string when it is null or empty.

Signature

Check.failIfEmptyStr str

Parameters

Name Type Description
str string The string to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<string> A Check containing the empty or null string; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 "hello" |> Check.failIfEmptyStr // Ok "hello"
 "" |> Check.failIfEmptyStr // Error ()

item.title: Check.failIfEqual
item.description:
Returns success when the values are equal.

Signature

Check.failIfEqual expected actual

Parameters

Name Type Description
expected ‘a The expected value.
actual ‘a The actual value.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the values are equal; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 Check.failIfEqual 5 6 // Ok ()
 Check.failIfEqual 5 5 // Error ()

item.title: Check.failIfNone
item.description:
Returns the value when the option is Some.

Signature

Check.failIfNone opt

Parameters

Name Type Description
opt ‘a option The option to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<‘a> A Check containing the value if present; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 Some 5 |> Check.failIfNone // Ok 5
 None |> Check.failIfNone // Error ()
union case Option.Some: Value: 'T -> Option<'T>
union case Option.None: Option<'T>

item.title: Check.failIfNonEmptyStr
item.description:
Returns success when the string is null or empty.

Signature

Check.failIfNonEmptyStr str

Parameters

Name Type Description
str string The string to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the string is null or empty; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 "" |> Check.failIfNonEmptyStr // Ok ()
 "hello" |> Check.failIfNonEmptyStr // Error ()

item.title: Check.failIfNotBlank
item.description:
Returns success when the string is blank.

Signature

Check.failIfNotBlank str

Parameters

Name Type Description
str string The string to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the string is blank; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 "  " |> Check.failIfNotBlank // Ok ()
 "hello" |> Check.failIfNotBlank // Error ()

item.title: Check.failIfNotEmpty
item.description:
Returns success when the sequence is empty.

Signature

Check.failIfNotEmpty coll

Parameters

Name Type Description
coll ‘a seq The sequence to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the sequence is empty; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 [] |> Check.failIfNotEmpty // Ok ()
 [1] |> Check.failIfNotEmpty // Error ()

item.title: Check.failIfNotEqual
item.description:
Returns success when the values are not equal.

Signature

Check.failIfNotEqual expected actual

Parameters

Name Type Description
expected ‘a The expected value.
actual ‘a The actual value.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the values differ; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 Check.failIfNotEqual 5 5 // Ok ()
 Check.failIfNotEqual 5 6 // Error ()

item.title: Check.failIfNotNull
item.description:
Returns success when the value is null.

Signature

Check.failIfNotNull value

Parameters

Name Type Description
value ‘a The value to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the value is null; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 null |> Check.failIfNotNull // Ok ()
 "hello" |> Check.failIfNotNull // Error ()

item.title: Check.failIfNull
item.description:
Returns the value when it is null.

Signature

Check.failIfNull value

Parameters

Name Type Description
value ‘a The value to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<‘a> A Check containing the null value; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 null |> Check.failIfNull // Ok null
 "hello" |> Check.failIfNull // Error ()

item.title: Check.failIfSome
item.description:
Returns success when the option is None.

Signature

Check.failIfSome opt

Parameters

Name Type Description
opt ‘a option The option to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the option is None.

Examples

 None |> Check.failIfSome // Ok ()
 Some 5 |> Check.failIfSome // Error ()
union case Option.None: Option<'T>
union case Option.Some: Value: 'T -> Option<'T>

item.title: Check.failIfValueNone
item.description:
Returns the value when the value option is ValueSome.

Signature

Check.failIfValueNone opt

Parameters

Name Type Description
opt ‘a voption The value option to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<‘a> A Check containing the value if present; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 ValueSome 5 |> Check.failIfValueNone // Ok 5
 ValueNone |> Check.failIfValueNone // Error ()
union case ValueOption.ValueSome: 'T -> ValueOption<'T>
union case ValueOption.ValueNone: ValueOption<'T>

item.title: Check.failIfValueSome
item.description:
Returns success when the value option is ValueNone.

Signature

Check.failIfValueSome opt

Parameters

Name Type Description
opt ‘a voption The value option to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the value option is ValueNone.

Examples

 ValueNone |> Check.failIfValueSome // Ok ()
 ValueSome 5 |> Check.failIfValueSome // Error ()
union case ValueOption.ValueNone: ValueOption<'T>
union case ValueOption.ValueSome: 'T -> ValueOption<'T>

item.title: Check.fromPredicate
item.description:
Builds a check from a predicate while preserving the successful value.

Signature

Check.fromPredicate predicate value

Parameters

Name Type Description
predicate ‘value -> bool A function of type 'value -> bool to test the value.
value ‘value The value of type 'value to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<‘value> A Check containing the value if the predicate succeeds; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 5 |> Check.fromPredicate (fun x -> x > 0) // Ok 5
 -1 |> Check.fromPredicate (fun x -> x > 0) // Error ()

item.title: Check.notBlank
item.description:
Returns the string when it is not blank.

Signature

Check.notBlank str

Parameters

Name Type Description
str string The string to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<string> A Check containing the non-blank string; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 "hello" |> Check.notBlank // Ok "hello"
 "  " |> Check.notBlank // Error ()

item.title: Check.notEmpty
item.description:
Returns the sequence when it is not empty.

Signature

Check.notEmpty coll

Parameters

Name Type Description
coll ‘a seq The sequence to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<‘a seq> A Check containing the non-empty sequence; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 [1] |> Check.notEmpty // Ok [1]
 [] |> Check.notEmpty // Error ()

item.title: Check.notEqual
item.description:
Returns success when the values are not equal.

Signature

Check.notEqual expected actual

Parameters

Name Type Description
expected ‘a The expected value.
actual ‘a The actual value.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the values differ; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 Check.notEqual 5 6 // Ok ()
 Check.notEqual 5 5 // Error ()

item.title: Check.notNull
item.description:
Returns the value when it is not null.

Signature

Check.notNull value

Parameters

Name Type Description
value ‘a The value to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<‘a> A Check containing the non-null value; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 "hello" |> Check.notNull // Ok "hello"
 null |> Check.notNull // Error ()

item.title: Check.okIf
item.description:
Returns success when the condition is true.

Signature

Check.okIf cond

Parameters

Name Type Description
cond bool The boolean condition to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if cond is true.

Examples

 Check.okIf (1 = 1) // Ok ()
 Check.okIf (1 = 2) // Error ()

item.title: Check.okIfBlank
item.description:
Returns success when the string is blank.

Signature

Check.okIfBlank str

Parameters

Name Type Description
str string The string to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the string is null, empty, or whitespace; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 "  " |> Check.okIfBlank // Ok ()
 "hello" |> Check.okIfBlank // Error ()

item.title: Check.okIfEmpty
item.description:
Returns success when the sequence is empty.

Signature

Check.okIfEmpty coll

Parameters

Name Type Description
coll ‘a seq The sequence to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the sequence is empty; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 [] |> Check.okIfEmpty // Ok ()
 [1] |> Check.okIfEmpty // Error ()

item.title: Check.okIfEmptyStr
item.description:
Returns success when the string is null or empty.

Signature

Check.okIfEmptyStr str

Parameters

Name Type Description
str string The string to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the string is null or empty; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 "" |> Check.okIfEmptyStr // Ok ()
 "hello" |> Check.okIfEmptyStr // Error ()

item.title: Check.okIfEqual
item.description:
Returns success when the values are equal.

Signature

Check.okIfEqual expected actual

Parameters

Name Type Description
expected ‘a The expected value.
actual ‘a The actual value.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the values are equal; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 Check.okIfEqual 5 5 // Ok ()
 Check.okIfEqual 5 6 // Error ()

item.title: Check.okIfNone
item.description:
Returns success when the option is None.

Signature

Check.okIfNone opt

Parameters

Name Type Description
opt ‘a option The option to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the option is None.

Examples

 None |> Check.okIfNone // Ok ()
 Some 5 |> Check.okIfNone // Error ()
union case Option.None: Option<'T>
union case Option.Some: Value: 'T -> Option<'T>

item.title: Check.okIfNonEmptyStr
item.description:
Returns the string when it is not null or empty.

Signature

Check.okIfNonEmptyStr str

Parameters

Name Type Description
str string The string to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<string> A Check containing the non-empty string; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 "hello" |> Check.okIfNonEmptyStr // Ok "hello"
 "" |> Check.okIfNonEmptyStr // Error ()

item.title: Check.okIfNotBlank
item.description:
Returns the string when it is not blank.

Signature

Check.okIfNotBlank str

Parameters

Name Type Description
str string The string to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<string> A Check containing the non-blank string; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 "hello" |> Check.okIfNotBlank // Ok "hello"
 "  " |> Check.okIfNotBlank // Error ()

item.title: Check.okIfNotEmpty
item.description:
Returns the sequence when it is not empty.

Signature

Check.okIfNotEmpty coll

Parameters

Name Type Description
coll ‘a seq The sequence of type seq<'a> to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<‘a seq> A Check containing the non-empty sequence; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 [1; 2] |> Check.okIfNotEmpty // Ok [1; 2]
 [] |> Check.okIfNotEmpty // Error ()

item.title: Check.okIfNotEqual
item.description:
Returns success when the values are not equal.

Signature

Check.okIfNotEqual expected actual

Parameters

Name Type Description
expected ‘a The expected value.
actual ‘a The actual value.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the values differ; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 Check.okIfNotEqual 5 6 // Ok ()
 Check.okIfNotEqual 5 5 // Error ()

item.title: Check.okIfNotNull
item.description:
Returns the value when it is not null.

Signature

Check.okIfNotNull value

Parameters

Name Type Description
value ‘a The value of type 'a to check for null.

Returns

Type Description
Check<‘a> A Check containing the non-null value; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 "hello" |> Check.okIfNotNull // Ok "hello"
 null |> Check.okIfNotNull // Error ()

item.title: Check.okIfNull
item.description:
Returns success when the value is null.

Signature

Check.okIfNull value

Parameters

Name Type Description
value ‘a The value to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the value is null; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 null |> Check.okIfNull // Ok ()
 "hello" |> Check.okIfNull // Error ()

item.title: Check.okIfSome
item.description:
Returns the value when the option is Some.

Signature

Check.okIfSome opt

Parameters

Name Type Description
opt ‘a option The option to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<‘a> A Check containing the value if present; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 Some 5 |> Check.okIfSome // Ok 5
 None |> Check.okIfSome // Error ()
union case Option.Some: Value: 'T -> Option<'T>
union case Option.None: Option<'T>

item.title: Check.okIfValueNone
item.description:
Returns success when the value option is ValueNone.

Signature

Check.okIfValueNone opt

Parameters

Name Type Description
opt ‘a voption The value option to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<unit> A Check that succeeds if the value option is ValueNone.

Examples

 ValueNone |> Check.okIfValueNone // Ok ()
 ValueSome 5 |> Check.okIfValueNone // Error ()
union case ValueOption.ValueNone: ValueOption<'T>
union case ValueOption.ValueSome: 'T -> ValueOption<'T>

item.title: Check.okIfValueSome
item.description:
Returns the value when the value option is ValueSome.

Signature

Check.okIfValueSome opt

Parameters

Name Type Description
opt ‘a voption The voption to check.

Returns

Type Description
Check<‘a> A Check containing the value if present; otherwise, an Error with unit.

Examples

 ValueSome 5 |> Check.okIfValueSome // Ok 5
 ValueNone |> Check.okIfValueSome // Error ()
union case ValueOption.ValueSome: 'T -> ValueOption<'T>
union case ValueOption.ValueNone: ValueOption<'T>

item.title: Check.orError
item.description:
Maps a unit error into the supplied application error value.

Signature

Check.orError error result

Parameters

Name Type Description
error ‘error The domain error of type 'error to return on failure.
result Check<‘value> The source Check.

Returns

Type Description
Result<‘value, ’error> A Result with the provided error value.

Remarks

This is the primary bridge from checks to domain-specific results.

Examples

 "" |> Check.okIfNonEmptyStr |> Check.orError "Empty string" // Error "Empty string"
 "hello" |> Check.okIfNonEmptyStr |> Check.orError "Empty string" // Ok "hello"

item.title: Check.orErrorWith
item.description:
Maps a unit error into an application error produced on demand.

Signature

Check.orErrorWith errorFn result

Parameters

Name Type Description
errorFn unit -> ’error A function of type unit -> 'error to produce the error.
result Check<‘value> The source Check.

Returns

Type Description
Result<‘value, ’error> A Result with the produced error value.

Examples

 "" |> Check.okIfNonEmptyStr |> Check.orErrorWith (fun () -> "Empty string") // Error "Empty string"
